message:
perf(lexical): split large cass writer batches into parallel submissions across the Tantivy worker pool

Follow-up to the recent CASS_MAX_WRITER_THREADS bump and the
`Writer::run` migration.  Even with 32 indexer threads available,
a single giant `Writer::run(...)` submission was visibly
serializing inside Tantivy: the threads woke up, drained the
batch, and then sat idle while the producer prepared the next
giant batch.  The producer side (rayon `par_iter` doc construction)
was independently parallel but couldn't overlap with the writer
because the entire batch had to materialize before the single
`run()` call.

This change introduces an explicit "parallel add plan" that
splits a doc batch into multiple smaller submissions consumed
concurrently by the writer pool.

New constants + env knobs
-------------------------
  CASS_PARALLEL_ADD_MIN_DOCS         = 2_048
    `CASS_TANTIVY_PARALLEL_ADD_MIN_DOCS`
    Below this size, fall back to the existing single-batch path
    (rayon prep overhead would dominate).

  CASS_PARALLEL_ADD_TARGET_BATCH_DOCS = 512
    `CASS_TANTIVY_PARALLEL_ADD_BATCH_DOCS`
    Target docs per writer submission — small enough that the
    writer pool keeps moving, large enough that lock-acquisition
    cost stays amortized.

  CASS_PARALLEL_ADD_MAX_BATCHES       = 64
    `CASS_TANTIVY_PARALLEL_ADD_MAX_BATCHES`
    Hard cap on the number of submissions per `add_documents`
    call so we don't fan out to a million tiny chunks under a
    pathologically large input.

`cass_parallel_add_plan(doc_count, parallelism, target_batch,
min_parallel_docs, max_batches)` returns a `CassAddPlan { parallel_batches,
batch_docs, batch_count }`.  Logic:

  - 0 docs → no-op plan.
  - Compute `batch_count = doc_count.div_ceil(target_batch)
                          .min(max_batches)
                          .min(parallelism * 2)
                          .max(1)`.
    The `parallelism * 2` ceiling matches the typical "2× cores
    in flight" sweet spot for Tantivy's writer queue depth.
  - `batch_docs = doc_count.div_ceil(batch_count)` redistributes
    the docs evenly across batches.
  - Engage parallel submission only when
    `doc_count >= min_parallel_docs && batch_count > 1`.

CassTantivyIndex::add_documents
-------------------------------
When the plan engages parallel batches:
  `docs.par_chunks(batch_docs).try_for_each(|chunk| {
       let prepared: Vec<TantivyDocument> = chunk.iter().map(...).collect();
       writer.run(prepared.into_iter().map(UserOperation::Add))?;
       Ok(())
   })?;`

Each rayon worker prepares + submits its own chunk independently,
overlapping prep with submission across the pool.  When the plan
declines (small batches), we keep the existing single-shot
`run()` path.

Each call now logs the chosen plan
(`docs`, `batch_docs`, `batch_count`, `parallel_batches`,
`writer_parallelism`) at debug level so operators can verify the
fanout matches their host's parallelism without rebuilding.

Test
----
`cass_parallel_add_plan_enables_parallel_submission_for_large_batches`
exercises three regimes:
  - 4_096 docs / 8 cores: parallel, 8 × 512.
  - 200_000 docs / 32 cores: parallel, 64 × 3_125 (capped by
    max_batches at 64).
  - 1_024 docs / 8 cores: sequential (below
    min_parallel_docs threshold) but still planned at 2 × 512
    so a future env-tweak can flip it on without touching the
    code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
